---
title: "ggpaintr Manual Test Workbook"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

This workbook is the canonical interactive manual test guide for the current
`ggpaintr` workflow. It is intended to complement, not replace, the
package-standard automated tests in `tests/testthat/`.

It aligns with:

- `working_scripts/notes/testing-strategy.md`
- `tests/manual/manual-checklist-ggpaintr.md`

Current automated coverage for the new error-feedback channel already includes:

- malformed `expr` handling in `paintr_complete_expr_safe()`
- invalid upload handling in `paintr_complete_expr_safe()`
- plot-stage missing-object handling in `paintr_get_plot_safe()`
- successful runtime assembly in `paintr_build_runtime()`
- exported app wiring for `paintr_build_runtime()` and `outputError`

See:

- `tests/testthat/test-runtime-feedback.R`
- `tests/testthat/test-export-shiny.R`

## How To Use This Workbook

Use this file from an interactive RStudio session.

1. Run the setup chunk first.
2. Review the printed fixture paths.
3. Run one app chunk at a time to launch `ggpaintr_app()`.
4. Close the current app before launching the next one.
5. Use the expected-check lists below to record pass/fail behavior.

## Error Feedback Feature Test Guide

Use the scenarios below to test the dedicated inline error channel end to end.

| Scenario | Trigger | Expected error label | Plot behavior | Code behavior |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Completion-stage error | malformed `expr` such as `~` | `Input error:` | plot clears / stays empty | code stays blank |
| Upload validation error | upload `bad_extension.txt` | `Input error:` | plot stays empty | code stays blank |
| Plot-stage error | missing object such as `unknown_object` | `Plot error:` | plot clears / stays empty | code remains visible |
| Recovery after error | correct the inputs and redraw | no error panel | valid plot renders | code updates normally |
| Exported app error behavior | run exported app and trigger one error case | same inline error as in-repo app | same clearing behavior | same code behavior |

## Setup

Assumptions:

- Run this from the repo root.
- Use the maintained package path centered on `R/paintr-app.R`, `R/paintr-parse.R`, `R/paintr-runtime.R`, `R/paintr-ui.R`, and `R/paintr-upload.R`.
- Use the committed fixture files under `tests/testthat/fixtures/` for upload checks.

```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(ggpaintr)
library(shiny)
library(tidyverse)
library(shinyWidgets)
library(palmerpenguins)
library(rlang)
library(assertthat)
library(lobstr)
library(here)

world_map <- map_data("world")

# Some EU Contries
some.eu.countries <- c(
  "Portugal", "Spain", "France", "Switzerland", "Germany",
  "Austria", "Belgium", "UK", "Netherlands",
  "Denmark", "Poland", "Italy",
  "Croatia", "Slovenia", "Hungary", "Slovakia",
  "Czech republic"
)
# Retrievethe map data
some.eu.maps <- map_data("world", region = some.eu.countries)

# Compute the centroid as the mean longitude and lattitude
# Used as label coordinate for country's names
region.lab.data <- some.eu.maps %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(long = mean(long), lat = mean(lat))


csv_fixture <- here("tests", "testthat", "fixtures", "simple_numeric.csv")
rds_fixture <- here("tests", "testthat", "fixtures", "simple_numeric.rds")
bad_fixture <- here("tests", "testthat", "fixtures", "bad_extension.txt")

list(
  csv_fixture = csv_fixture,
  rds_fixture = rds_fixture,
  bad_fixture = bad_fixture
)
```

Upload fixture paths to use during manual testing:

- `r csv_fixture`
- `r rds_fixture`
- `r bad_fixture`

Recommended RStudio flow:

- restart R if you have an older `ggpaintr` namespace loaded
- run the setup chunk
- launch one app chunk
- perform the listed checks
- close the app window before continuing

## Formula 1: Static All-Placeholders Interaction

This app is the main static-data interaction sweep. It exercises `var`, `text`, `num`, `expr`, `outputCode`, and layer checkbox behavior in one place.

```{r formula-1, eval=FALSE}
ggpaintr_app("
ggplot(data = iris, aes(x = var, y = var)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = var), size = num, alpha = num) +
  labs(title = text, x = text, y = text) +
  facet_wrap(expr) +
  theme(legend.position = text)
")
```

Suggested manual inputs:

- `x = Sepal.Length`
- `y = Sepal.Width`
- `color = Species`
- `size = 2.5`
- `alpha = 0.7`
- `title = Iris manual test`
- `x text = Sepal length`
- `y text = Sepal width`
- `facet_wrap expr = ~ Species`
- `legend.position text = bottom`

Expected checks:

- Control tabs render for all layers.
- Clicking `draw` updates both the plot and `outputCode`.
- Clearing `title` removes `labs(title = ...)` content from the generated code.
- Clearing `facet_wrap` removes the faceting call from the generated code.
- Clearing `legend.position` removes that theme argument from the generated code.
- Unchecking `geom_point`, `labs`, `facet_wrap`, or `theme` removes that layer from both the plot and the code.

## Formula 2: `var` Expression Input

This app checks the current behavior where `var` accepts expression-like input, not just plain column names.

```{r formula-2, eval=FALSE}
ggpaintr_app("
ggplot(data = mtcars, aes(x = log(var), y = var + 1)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = text)
")
```

Suggested manual inputs:

- `x = log(mpg)`
- `y = disp + 1`
- `title = var expression test`

Expected checks:

- The plot draws successfully with expression-like `var` inputs.
- `outputCode` contains `log(mpg)` for `x`.
- `outputCode` contains `disp + 1` for `y`.

## Formula 3: Global Upload

This app checks upload-backed global data, delayed `var` controls, default/custom dataset names, and unsupported file handling.

```{r formula-3, eval=FALSE}
ggpaintr_app("
ggplot(data = upload, aes(x = var, y = var)) +
  geom_point(size = num) +
  labs(title = 'good')
")
```

Suggested manual sequence:

- Launch the app and confirm the `var` selectors are not available before an upload.
- Upload `simple_numeric.csv` from `tests/testthat/fixtures/simple_numeric.csv`.
- Leave the dataset object name blank the first time.
- Use `x = x`, `y = y`, `size = 2`, `title = uploaded scatter`.
- Click `draw`.
- Repeat with `simple_numeric.rds` from `tests/testthat/fixtures/simple_numeric.rds`.
- Repeat again with custom dataset object name `manual_upload_data`.
- Retry with `bad_extension.txt` from `tests/testthat/fixtures/bad_extension.txt`.

Expected checks:

- `var` selectors appear only after a valid upload.
- With a blank dataset name, the generated code uses a filename-derived object name.
- With a custom dataset name, the generated code uses `manual_upload_data`.
- The app draws successfully for both `.csv` and `.rds`.
- The bad-extension upload shows a clear inline error without crashing the session.
- When upload completion fails, the plot does not render.

## Formula 4: Layer-Specific Upload

This app checks mixed static/upload layering and confirms that upload-backed `var` controls apply only to the upload-backed layer.

```{r formula-4, eval=FALSE}
ggpaintr_app("
ggplot(data = iris, aes(x = Sepal.Length, y = Sepal.Width)) +
  geom_point(color = 'grey70', size = 2) +
  geom_point(data = upload, aes(x = var, y = var, color = var), size = num) +
  labs(title = text)
")
```

Suggested manual sequence:

- Launch the app and inspect the controls before upload.
- Confirm the static `iris` layer does not need upload-driven controls.
- Upload `simple_numeric.csv` or `simple_numeric.rds`.
- Set upload-layer `x = x`, `y = y`, `color = group`, `size = 3`.
- Set `title = mixed static/upload plot`.
- Click `draw`.
- Uncheck the upload-backed `geom_point` layer and draw again.

Expected checks:

- Only the upload-backed layer depends on uploaded columns.
- The static `iris` layer remains intact before and after upload.
- The combined plot shows both static and uploaded layers when enabled.
- Disabling the upload-backed layer leaves a valid static plot.

## Formula 5: Export Smoke Test

This app is the static export-oriented formula. Use it along with Formula 3 for the two export checks.

```{r formula-5, eval=FALSE}
ggpaintr_app("
ggplot(data = iris, aes(x = var, y = var)) +
  geom_point(size = num) +
  labs(title = text, subtitle = text) +
  facet_grid(var ~ var, labeller = expr)
")
```

Suggested manual inputs:

- `x = Sepal.Length`
- `y = Sepal.Width`
- `row facet = Species`
- `col facet = Species`
- `labeller expr = label_both`
- `size = 1.5`
- `title = export test`
- `subtitle = static formula export`

Expected checks:

- The static app draws and updates `outputCode` correctly.
- Export the generated app from this formula and run it.
- Export the generated app from Formula 3 and run it.
- In the exported upload app, repeat one CSV upload flow and one RDS upload flow.
- Confirm the exported apps still render both the plot and the generated code.
- Confirm exported apps also show the same inline error message behavior on bad `expr` or bad upload input.

## Formula 6: Error Feedback Checks

Use these short apps to confirm the new draw-time error path for malformed expressions, invalid uploads, missing objects, and recovery after failure.

```{r formula-6a, eval=FALSE}
ggpaintr_app("
ggplot(data = iris, aes(x = Sepal.Length, y = Sepal.Width)) +
  geom_point() +
  facet_wrap(expr)
")
```

Suggested manual sequence:

- Enter `~` for `facet_wrap expr`.
- Click `draw`.
- Replace it with `~ Speciesasdf`.
- Click `draw` again.

Expected checks:

- The app shows an inline `Input error:` message for bare `~`.
- The session keeps running.
- No stale plot is rendered.
- `outputCode` stays blank because expression completion failed.
- With `~ Speciesasdf`, the inline error channel shows a `Plot error:` message mentioning `Speciesasdf`.
- The plot pane does not show raw Shiny `Error: [object Object]`.
- `outputCode` remains visible for the `~ Speciesasdf` case because completion succeeded before render-time failure.

```{r formula-6-upload-error, eval=FALSE}
ggpaintr_app("
ggplot(data = upload, aes(x = var, y = var)) +
  geom_point()
")
```

Suggested manual sequence:

- Launch the app.
- Upload `bad_extension.txt`.
- Click `draw`.

Expected checks:

- The app shows an inline `Input error:` message.
- Upload-backed `var` selectors do not become usable from the invalid file.
- The plot stays empty.
- `outputCode` stays blank.

```{r formula-6b, eval=FALSE}
ggpaintr_app("
ggplot(data = unknown_object, aes(x = mpg, y = disp)) +
  geom_point()
")
```

Suggested manual sequence:

- Launch the app.
- Click `draw`.

Expected checks:

- The app launches without crashing before interaction.
- The app shows an inline `Plot error:` message mentioning `unknown_object`.
- The plot stays empty.
- The generated code remains visible even though plot construction failed.

```{r formula-6c-recovery, eval=FALSE}
ggpaintr_app("
ggplot(data = iris, aes(x = Sepal.Length, y = Sepal.Width)) +
  geom_point() +
  facet_wrap(expr)
")
```

Suggested manual sequence:

- First enter `~` for `facet_wrap expr` and click `draw`.
- Confirm the inline `Input error:` appears and the plot stays empty.
- Replace the input with `~ Species`.
- Click `draw` again.

Expected checks:

- The previous error message disappears after the successful draw.
- The plot renders normally after recovery.
- `outputCode` updates to the valid faceting code.
- The session remains usable without restart.

## Exported App Error Feedback Check

Use Formula 6A or the upload-error app above, then export the generated app and run it.

Suggested manual sequence:

- Export the app after opening it in the workbook.
- Run the exported app.
- Trigger one malformed `expr` error or one invalid upload error.
- Correct the input and redraw.

Expected checks:

- The exported app shows the same inline error message pattern as the in-repo app.
- The plot clears on failure and renders again after recovery.
- `outputCode` stays blank for completion-stage errors and updates normally after recovery.

## Final Checklist

- Plot updates after clicking `draw`.
- `outputCode` matches the active inputs.
- Blank optional `text`, `num`, and `expr` values disappear cleanly from generated code where expected.
- Unchecked layers disappear from both the plot and `outputCode`.
- Upload-backed `var` controls appear only after valid upload.
- Default upload naming works when the dataset name box is blank.
- Custom upload naming works when a dataset name is provided.
- Inline errors appear for malformed `expr`, bad upload types, and missing objects.
- The plot clears and does not stay stale after an error.
- Generated code remains visible when plot construction fails after completion succeeds.
- Recovery after an error clears the error panel and restores normal plotting.
- Exported apps run and preserve the same behavior for static and upload formulas.
